title: Goldilocks and the Other Three Bears

q.en: Why was the young girl called Goldilocks?
q.zh: 小女孩为什么叫“金发姑娘”（Goldilocks）？

options.en: Because she lived in a golden house
options.zh: 因为她住在金色的房子里

options.en: Because her beautiful locks glistened in the sun like gold
options.zh: 因为她美丽的发绺在阳光下像金子一样闪闪发光

options.en: Because she wore a gold lock on her door according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 因为她门上挂了一把金锁（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Because she loved gold jewelry

explain.en: The book says, "She was called Goldilocks because her beautiful locks glistened in the sun, like gold."
explain.zh: 书中写道：“人们叫她金发姑娘，因为她美丽的发绺在阳光下像金子一样闪闪发光。”

q.en: Who lived in the cozy cottage in the woods?
q.zh: 森林里那座舒适的小屋住着谁？

options.en: A teeny baby Bear, a medium mama Bear, and a huge papa Bear
options.zh: 一只极小的熊宝宝、中等身材的熊妈妈和巨大的熊爸爸

options.en: A family of rabbits according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 一家兔子（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: Three goldfish
An old woodcutter

explain.en: The book says, "The family was a teeny-sized baby Bear, a medium-sized mama Bear, and a huge-sized papa Bear."
explain.zh: 书中说：“这家人是一只极小的熊宝宝、中等身材的熊妈妈和巨大的熊爸爸。”

q.en: Why did the Bear family go blueberry picking every morning?
q.zh: 熊一家为什么每天早上去采蓝莓？

options.en: To pick flowers
So the porridge had time to cool
Because they were hungry for blueberries
To meet their friends

explain.en: The book says, "After Papa Bear made a steaming pot of porridge, the family went blueberry picking. This way, the porridge had time to cool."
explain.zh: 书中说：“熊爸爸煮了一锅热粥后，全家去采蓝莓。这样粥就有时间凉下来。”

q.en: How does the book describe Papa Bear's porridge when Goldilocks tastes it?
q.zh: 金发姑娘尝熊爸爸的粥时，书里怎么形容它？

options.en: As hot as lava
Just right
As sweet as honey
As cold as gazpacho

explain.en: Goldilocks tastes Papa's porridge and yelps, "This porridge is as hot as lava!"
explain.zh: 金发姑娘尝了熊爸爸的粥后叫道：“这粥热得像熔岩！”

q.en: Which bowl of porridge did Goldilocks eat up completely?
q.zh: 金发姑娘把哪一碗粥吃得一干二净？

options.en: Mama Bear's medium bowl according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 熊妈妈的中碗（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: None of them
Papa Bear's huge bowl
Teeny Baby Bear's small bowl

explain.en: The book says, "she quickly gobbled up every last bit of Teeny Baby Bear's porridge."
explain.zh: 书中写道：“她飞快地把熊宝宝粥碗里的最后一口也吞了下去。”

q.en: What happened to Teeny Baby Bear's small chair?
q.zh: 熊宝宝的小椅子怎么了？

options.en: It grew bigger
It broke into hundreds of pieces
It disappeared
It turned to gold according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 它变成了金色（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Goldilocks rocks in the small chair and it goes "Crack!"—later the baby bear says it's "broken in hundreds of pieces!"
explain.zh: 金发姑娘在小椅子上摇晃，发出“咔嚓！”声——后来熊宝宝说椅子“碎成了几百块！”

q.en: When the three Bears find Goldilocks asleep, what does Teeny Baby Bear say about the bed?
q.zh: 三只熊发现金发姑娘睡着时，熊宝宝对那张床说了什么？

options.en: "The bed is empty"
"My bed is too hard"
"Someone's been sleeping in my bed... And she's still there!"
options.zh: “有人睡过我的床……而且她还在这儿！”

options.en: "My bed is made perfectly" according to some versions of the story
options.zh: “我的床铺得整整齐齐”（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says, "Someone's been sleeping in my bed," growled Teeny Baby Bear... "And she's still there!"
explain.zh: 书中说，熊宝宝咆哮道：“有人睡过我的床……而且她还在这儿！”

q.en: Across the porridge, chairs, and beds, what pattern did Goldilocks find?
q.zh: 从粥、椅子到床，金发姑娘发现了什么规律？

options.en: All three Bears' things were exactly the same size according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 三只熊的东西尺寸完全一样（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: Only Papa's things were 'just right'
options.zh: 只有熊爸爸的东西“刚刚好”

options.en: The smallest things were always too small
Papa's were too big/hot/hard, Mama's also not right, and only Teeny's were 'just right'
options.zh: 熊爸爸的太大/太热/太硬，熊妈妈的也不合适，只有熊宝宝的“刚刚好”

explain.en: The book repeats the pattern: Papa's porridge is "as hot as lava," Mama's "as cold as gazpacho," and Teeny's "just right"—the same with chairs and beds.
explain.zh: 书中反复出现这一规律：熊爸爸的粥“热得像熔岩”，熊妈妈的“冷得像凉汤”，熊宝宝的“刚刚好”——椅子和床也一样。

q.en: Why does the author have each bear growl 'Someone's been eating my porridge'?
q.zh: 作者为什么让每只熊都咆哮“有人吃过我的粥”？

options.en: To describe the forest
To explain how porridge is made according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 为了解释粥是怎么做的（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: To build a repeating, playful pattern as each bear notices
options.zh: 为了营造重复的、俏皮的节奏，表现每只熊的发现

options.en: To show the bears were happy

explain.en: The author repeats the line with huge, medium, and teeny growls—"Someone's been eating my porridge"—creating a rhythmic pattern that highlights each bear's discovery.
explain.zh: 作者用大、中、小三种咆哮声重复这句话——“有人吃过我的粥”——形成有节奏的规律，凸显每只熊的发现。

q.en: At the end, what does Goldilocks remember as 'just right'?
q.zh: 结尾处，金发姑娘把什么记成“刚刚好”？

options.en: The porridge, the little rocking chair, and the comfy cozy bed
options.zh: 那碗粥、那把小摇椅和那张舒适温暖的床

options.en: The long staircase
The broken chair pieces according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 那些椅子碎片（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: The blueberry bushes

explain.en: The book says, "she did often daydream about that delicious bowl of porridge, and that perfect little rocking chair, and that comfy, cozy bed. They really were just right."
explain.zh: 书中说：“她常会梦到那碗美味的粥、那把完美的小摇椅和那张舒适温暖的床。它们真的都刚刚好。”

prompt.en: Goldilocks went into the Bears' empty cottage, ate their porridge, broke a chair, and slept in a bed—without asking. Have you ever been curious and done something you shouldn't have? What happened, and what did you learn about respecting others' things?
prompt.zh: 金发姑娘未经允许走进了三只熊空无一人的小屋，喝了它们的粥、弄坏了椅子、睡了床。你有没有也曾因好奇做了不该做的事？结果如何？你从中学到了关于尊重他人物品的什么道理？

en: Goldilocks was curious by nature and went in without permission.
She ate, sat, and slept in the Bears' things.
The Bears were surprised and upset when they returned.
Think about a time you learned to respect others' property.
zh: 金发姑娘天性好奇，未经允许就进去了。
她吃了、坐了、睡了熊一家的东西。
想想你学会尊重他人财物的一次经历。
